task_recall.notes: Both primary tasks (Tandoor and Generator enquiries) are correctly identified and surfaced as separate parent tasks. Implicit subtasks for supplier quoting are also added proactively. The only minor gap is that the Generator acknowledgement gap is flagged in the flags section but not elevated as its own explicit subtask, though this is a minor omission rather than a significant miss.

entity_accuracy.notes: Client A is correctly linked to both orders. Items (Tandoor and Generator) are accurately assigned to their respective threads and parent tasks. Ashish is correctly identified as the responsible party. Source threads are properly attributed separately to Thread 1 and Thread 2.

cross_thread_correlation.notes: The agent correctly keeps the two orders separate despite them being from the same client. It does not conflate the Tandoor and Generator orders, which is the core requirement of this test case. Source thread attribution is clear and distinct. The agent explicitly notes that no ambiguous correlations exist and that the tasks are clearly distinct items.

ambiguity_flagging.notes: The agent includes a dedicated ambiguity section and flags relevant operational uncertainties (missing specs, unacknowledged Generator request). The explicit statement that no ambiguous correlations exist is appropriate and helpful for human review. The flag about Ashish not having acknowledged the Generator request is a good operational catch. Could have been slightly more explicit about whether the two threads were verified as being from the same Client A entity, but this is a minor point.

passes: Two distinct parent tasks created for Tandoor and Generator orders respectively — core pass criterion met
Items are not grouped under a single order despite being from the same client
Correct source thread attribution: Thread 1 for Tandoor, Thread 2 for Generator
Client A correctly linked to both separate parent tasks
Implicit supplier quoting subtasks proactively identified for both orders
Ambiguity section explicitly and correctly states no cross-thread correlation confusion exists
Operational flag raised about Generator request lacking explicit acknowledgement from Ashish
Structured and readable output with clear hierarchy
failures: The unacknowledged Generator request is flagged in the warnings section but not converted into a dedicated follow-up subtask (minor gap)
No explicit verification note that Client A in Thread 1 and Client A in Thread 2 are confirmed to be the same entity — could be slightly more explicit about this assumption
notes: The agent handles this test case well. The primary requirement — keeping Tandoor and Generator orders as separate parent tasks for the same client — is fully satisfied. The output is well-structured, entity assignments are accurate, and the agent proactively adds implicit tasks. The ambiguity section correctly identifies that no conflation has occurred. Minor deductions for not converting the Generator acknowledgement gap into an actionable subtask and for not explicitly noting the assumption that both threads belong to the same Client A. Overall a strong, operationally useful output.
